what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that registers an image asset against the design-source channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter contains id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
File body after frontmatter is empty
standouts: The target URI points to a specific image asset: image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-10
It is tied to the 'design-source' channel
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Includes an ISO 8601 timestamp for when the attachment was added
concerns: The attachment has no title, caption, or descriptive label
No author or attaching context is recorded
The meaning of 'p-10' and its relationship to other assets is not explained in this file
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that links a single presentation image to the design-source channel.
questions: What does the image at p-10 depict?
Why was this image attached to the design-source channel?
Is p-10 part of a larger presentation sequence?
Who added this attachment and for what purpose?
purpose_vs_actual: The file records an image attachment for the design-source channel, which matches the purpose implied by its metadata fields.
intention_signals: To catalog an image asset reference within a channel memory system so it can be retrieved and associated with the design-source channel context.
contribution: It contributes a precise, timestamped image attachment reference (target image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-10) tied to the design-source channel, preserving asset linkage independently of the asset content.
summary_for_embedding: This is a channel attachment metadata file that records an image asset (target image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-10) attached to the design-source channel on 2026-06-22.
